title: Management Consulting Problem-Solving Methodologies
description: Standardized, structured frameworks and problem-solving cycles employed by top-tier global consulting firms to analyze, model, and optimize corporate client operations.

models.name: MECE Framework
models.definition: Mutually Exclusive, Collectively Exhaustive. A structuring principle that ensures a complex business problem is broken down into non-overlapping sub-problems that cover all possible aspects.
models.application: When building financial risk indices or operations dashboards, MECE ensures all cost categories are mapped without duplicates.

models.name: Issue Tree Structuring
models.definition: A visual breakdown of a complex business question into component parts, moving from the core 'Why?' down to testable operational hypotheses.
models.application: Utilized by consultants to trace customer churn spikes back to specific variables (e.g. support calls or shipping delays).

models.name: Pyramid Principle
models.definition: A communication framework created by Barbara Minto where recommendations are presented first, followed by supporting arguments and granular data points.
models.application: Serves as the structural guideline for our automated Executive Consulting Summary and dynamic board PDF compilers.
